title: Add --label2annotation flag
body:
Containerfile and Dockerfile have not way to describe Annotations.

--ltoa flat tells buildah to convert all Labels specified in the Containerfile to Annotations when generating OCI Image format.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4351

LGTM once the label name is sorted
I like `--annotate-labels` but `--label2annotation` is also nice, fine with whatever gets more votes.
What's the expectation if the base image has labels set on it?
I think the Labels in the base image are just carried forward as labels.

> I think the Labels in the base image are just carried forward as labels.

Can the tests confirm the behavior?

And is that what a person using this option would expect?  I don't want to lock in behavior that we might decide to change later.
It is what I would expect. And the test already confirms it.
How?  The base image used in the tests doesn't include any labels of its own that might or might not be carried through to an image based on it that was built with the new option.
I answered a different question then you asked.  I thought you were asking if --label2annotation test checked to make sure that annotations were stored and labels were not.
It does convert the image labels to Annotations. Not sure if this is right or wrong.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

PR needs rebase.
